name: Solyndra
summary: Solar panel maker that received a $535M DOE loan guarantee but collapsed when Chinese manufacturers crashed silicon and panel prices.
lesson: A capital-intensive hardware bet on a specific manufacturing process can be wiped out overnight by commodity price collapse.

name: Aquion Energy
summary: Maker of saltwater grid-storage batteries backed by Bill Gates and Kleiner Perkins that filed for bankruptcy after failing to reach profitable scale.
lesson: Novel battery chemistries need a credible path to cost parity with lithium-ion before scaling factories.

name: Beacon Power
summary: Flywheel energy storage company that filed for bankruptcy just weeks after Solyndra, another recipient of a DOE loan guarantee.
lesson: Being a pioneer in grid storage doesn't protect against running out of runway before the market matures.

name: Range Fuels
summary: Cellulosic ethanol producer that shut its Georgia plant after failing to convert wood waste into fuel at commercial scale.
lesson: Lab-scale conversion chemistry often does not translate cleanly to industrial-scale biofuel production.

name: KiOR
summary: Biofuel company that promised to turn wood chips into crude oil substitute but filed for bankruptcy after its Mississippi plant never hit production targets.
lesson: Overpromising production yields to investors and regulators eventually collides with physical and chemical reality.

name: Abound Solar
summary: Thin-film cadmium telluride solar panel maker that shut down after being unable to compete with falling silicon panel prices from China.
lesson: A cost advantage in thin-film solar evaporated once conventional silicon panel prices fell faster than expected.

name: Evergreen Solar
summary: Massachusetts solar wafer manufacturer that filed for bankruptcy after Chinese competitors undercut its production costs despite state subsidies.
lesson: Domestic manufacturing subsidies could not offset a structural cost disadvantage versus lower-cost overseas producers.

name: Better Place
summary: Israeli EV battery-swap network that raised nearly $1B but collapsed after failing to secure enough automaker partners and consumer adoption.
lesson: Building an entire infrastructure network before securing committed automaker and consumer demand is extremely high risk.

name: Northvolt
summary: Europe's flagship battery gigafactory startup filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection after production delays, quality issues, and a cash crunch despite billions raised.
lesson: Even massively funded gigafactory bets can fail if production ramp-up problems burn cash faster than customers can be delivered to.

name: Britishvolt
summary: UK battery gigafactory startup that collapsed into administration after failing to secure enough funding to build its planned plant.
lesson: Announcing a gigafactory before securing full construction financing leaves a company exposed to any funding gap.

name: Lion Electric
summary: Canadian electric school bus and truck maker that filed for bankruptcy protection after demand fell short of production capacity and cash reserves ran low.
lesson: Building manufacturing capacity ahead of firm, funded customer orders is a dangerous bet in commercial EVs.

name: Volta Industries
summary: EV charging network known for ad-supported free charging stalls that filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after failing to reach profitability post-SPAC merger.
lesson: An advertising-subsidized business model for capital-intensive charging infrastructure struggled to cover real hardware and site costs.

name: Sunnova Energy
summary: Residential solar and storage financing company that filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2025 after high interest rates and reduced federal incentives crushed its leveraged leasing model.
lesson: A debt-heavy leasing model for residential solar is highly exposed to rising interest rates and policy shifts.

name: SunPower
summary: Longtime residential solar installer and panel maker that filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2024 after liquidity problems and a collapsing dealer network.
lesson: Decades of market presence don't protect against a liquidity crisis when financing costs rise and demand softens.

name: Running Tide
summary: Ocean-based carbon removal startup backed by Shopify and Stripe that shut down after struggling to prove measurable, verifiable removal at scale.
lesson: Novel ocean carbon removal approaches face immense measurement and verification hurdles that can undercut buyer confidence.

name: Bolt Threads
summary: Biotech materials company behind Mylo mushroom-leather that paused production after brand partners delayed commitments, and later wound down.
lesson: Relying on a handful of brand partners for a novel material's commercial validation is risky if they can quietly walk away.

name: Katerra
summary: SoftBank-backed construction technology company aiming to industrialize sustainable modular building that filed for bankruptcy after massive overexpansion and losses.
lesson: Trying to vertically integrate an entire industry at once multiplies operational risk faster than it multiplies efficiency gains.

name: SunEdison
summary: Once the world's largest renewable energy developer, SunEdison filed for bankruptcy after an aggressive debt-fueled acquisition spree collapsed under its own weight.
lesson: Scaling a renewables developer through aggressive leverage and acquisitions can outrun the cash flow needed to service that debt.

name: Miasolé
summary: Thin-film solar panel maker that was sold at a steep discount to a Chinese conglomerate after burning through hundreds of millions without reaching profitability.
lesson: Being among the most heavily funded startups in a sector is no guarantee against a distressed fire-sale exit.

name: Nanosolar
summary: Thin-film CIGS solar panel maker, once one of the most hyped cleantech startups, that quietly shut down after years of production delays and missed targets.
lesson: Heavy pre-revenue hype and funding cannot substitute for solving hard manufacturing yield problems.

name: Beyond Meat
summary: Plant-based meat pioneer that went public at a high valuation but has suffered years of declining sales, heavy losses, and a collapsed stock price as demand plateaued.
lesson: Early hype and a successful IPO don't guarantee that consumer demand for a sustainability-driven product category will keep growing.

name: Watershed
summary: Enterprise carbon accounting and climate management software company that has become a category leader with major enterprise customers like Airbnb and Ramp.
lesson: Launching enterprise carbon accounting software right as corporate ESG disclosure requirements were tightening created strong pull demand.

name: Persefoni
summary: Carbon management and disclosure software platform that has scaled quickly serving large enterprises and financial institutions preparing for climate disclosure rules.
lesson: Financial-grade, audit-ready carbon accounting became a wedge into large enterprise and financial sector budgets.

name: Pachama
summary: Forest carbon monitoring company using satellite imagery and AI to verify carbon offset projects, backed by major investors and corporate partners like Airbnb and Microsoft.
lesson: Applying remote sensing and machine learning to verify forest carbon claims addressed a core credibility gap in the offset market.

name: CarbonCure Technologies
summary: Injects captured CO2 into concrete during mixing to both reduce emissions and strengthen the material; backed by Amazon's Climate Pledge Fund and deployed at hundreds of concrete plants.
lesson: A retrofit technology that improves an existing product's performance while cutting emissions removes adoption friction for a conservative industry.

name: Climeworks
summary: Swiss direct air capture pioneer operating the Orca and Mammoth DAC plants in Iceland, the largest operating DAC facilities in the world, with long-term offtake deals from Microsoft and others.
lesson: Being first to operate DAC at meaningful scale let Climeworks capture premium corporate removal contracts.

name: Carbon Engineering
summary: Canadian direct air capture technology company, backed early by Bill Gates, that was acquired by Occidental Petroleum in 2023 for over $1 billion to scale its DAC plants.
lesson: Partnering with an oil major for capital and deployment infrastructure gave a hard-tech DAC company a viable path to scale.

name: Twelve
summary: CO2 electrolysis company that converts captured carbon dioxide into sustainable jet fuel and chemicals, with partnerships including Alaska Airlines and Mercedes-Benz.
lesson: Targeting drop-in replacement fuels and materials made carbon utilization technology immediately usable by existing industrial customers.

name: Boston Metal
summary: MIT spinout developing molten oxide electrolysis to produce steel without fossil fuels, backed by Breakthrough Energy Ventures, moving toward commercial-scale green steel plants.
lesson: Tackling one of the hardest-to-decarbonize industrial sectors attracted patient, mission-aligned capital willing to fund a long hardware timeline.

name: Fervo Energy
summary: Enhanced geothermal energy company that has demonstrated commercially viable next-generation geothermal wells and signed major power purchase agreements with Google and utilities.
lesson: Adapting oil-and-gas drilling techniques to geothermal unlocked a scalable, always-on clean power source that utilities wanted immediately.

name: Heirloom
summary: Direct air capture startup using accelerated mineral carbonation on limestone, which opened the first commercial DAC facility in the US in 2023 and has offtake deals with Microsoft and Meta.
lesson: Using cheap, abundant minerals instead of exotic sorbents gave a newer DAC entrant a lower-cost path to first commercial deployment.

name: Charm Industrial
summary: Converts crop waste into bio-oil injected underground for durable carbon removal, and has signed large offtake deals with Microsoft, Stripe, and JPMorgan.
lesson: Focusing on a measurable, geologically durable removal pathway helped win credibility with sophisticated corporate carbon removal buyers.

name: SolarCity
summary: Residential solar installer and financing pioneer, co-founded by Elon Musk's cousins, that was acquired by Tesla in 2016 to form Tesla Energy.
lesson: Pioneering the solar lease financing model unlocked mass residential adoption and made the company a strategic acquisition target.

name: Sunrun
summary: Largest residential solar and battery storage company in the US, having grown through organic expansion and the Vivint Solar merger to serve hundreds of thousands of customers.
lesson: Combining solar-as-a-service financing with storage attachment created a durable, growing recurring-revenue business.

name: Bloom Energy
summary: Fuel cell power generation company that went public in 2018 and has become a supplier of on-site clean power to data centers and enterprises, including major AI data center deals.
lesson: Persisting through nearly two decades of R&D paid off once demand for reliable, on-site low-carbon power surged with data center growth.
